Hella expensive and it did not meet my expectations. Lol the worker tried to convince us to buy two lobster tails because as he put it "is like a piece of heaven"... idk about him but if that was his heaven then that's pretty sad. The lobster tail looked good don't get me wrong, but the taste and texture wasn't amaze balls. The outer shell was crunchy and hard, not flaky and soft. We also got an individual strawberry cheesecake with crumbs and for the size of my palm (and I have a small palm) you pay $10 for okay cheesecake. I wouldn't mind paying if it was a GOOD pastry but I did not think this was at all worth the money.

You had me at churro. I had the churro ice cream sandwich with Vietnamese coffee ice cream (topped with sweetened condensed milk!). I tried most of the flavors before making my decision and they were all delicious which made it really hard to choose. Honey cornbread and monkeybread were some of the others that piqued my interest. The staff was super friendly, helpful, and accommodating and didn't even get mad or annoyed when I accidentally knocked over a table and broke one of their flower pots (I'm ridiculously clumsy, especially when I'm focused on food). I can't wait to come back and try more of the weekly flavors! And super excited for Ramen Night!!
This is definitely a Pittsburgh staple! The ice cream is delicious. They're always adding new items and flavors to their menu which is co

Liberal geeks who should be working at Starbucks. Such sheep that they still believe in masks. But once u get past the high school juniors and seniors, the fact is...nothing special... truly nothing special

Excellent cheesecake. Order ahead from whole cheesecake... it's worth it! I've also had scones & muffins (trying not to eat cheesecake for breakfast!) which are also very good. Odd location between 2 neighborhoods but convenient from both & plenty of parking.

I go to a convention every year right down the street from this restaurant...and it is fantastic. The bread is made in house and is crusty and delicious. Their coffee is some of the best coffee I have ever had. Maybe I was just swept in the excitement of the convention, but it was fantastic. One negative...tiny coffee cups. I drink a lot of coffee and I felt like ordering 4 of the tiny cups and dumping them into a large mug. Size aside, the coffee was magical. Now, baked goods. I had two tarts while I was there and they were both fantastic. The macaroons were incredible. They had multiple flavors including a balsamic berry that was just this side of transcendent. And the sandwiches. I had a Croque Monsiour which was tasty, (I really wish they had the Croque Madame, because eggs make everything better.), the Croissant Pour Deux (small croissants layered with fig preserves, prosciutto and goat cheese), and the Seeded Roast Beef. All of their food was fantastic. We chose to keep going there rather than the expensive Brazilian Steak House down the street because their food was so spot on. I totally recommend this place to anyone in the area.

My current semi-permanent halfway-temporary home (mostly temporary actually) is Los Angeles, California. LA is cool. I had no urge to leave such a place. But I was able to take a break from the groovitude for a trip back to Ohio. LA to CLE? Umm, Cleveland is pretty bleak isn't it? Not so. I now realize that I love this town. Why did I have to go to LA and back to fully realize that? I have no idea. But I do love it. Cleveland has different vibes. It's different, not better or worse. Of course the reason for this speal of speech was Mitchell's Homemade Ice Cream. Wow. What a great place. Apparently this used to be a theater of some sort. So it's big. And white. Like ice cream. It's got a very clean design going on. Typical ice cream parlorish but with more character and more Cleveland. There's a long counter in front sporting all the ice cream flaves. Thirty or so of them. Immediately behind the counter is mucho glasso through which you can watch the ice cream makers hard at work. There's even a good sized upstairs with an even better view of the process. Is that the coolest part of it all? Of course not. That would be the ice cream. Why would you even ask? Yeah, homemade ice cream. Ain't that something. And that's not even a joke. I will speak for the Vanilla Bean and the Caramel Sea Salt. I've had ice cream of more taste and creativity at Piccadilly, but there's nothing like some good old homemade vanilla ice cream. The Caramel Sea Salt was salty; in case you didn't catch that already. It was very good though. Probably even worth the sugar and dairy. This place is CLE. This place is cool. And so is the ice cream because of it wasn't...it would suck.

I love ice cream! While in Vegas I had to try this place out. I wanted to try the soft serve and I am happy they offer samples. I wasn't crazy over the cereal milk, but I really liked fruity cereal milk. It taste just like Fruity Pebbles and had to try it with flakes. My soft serve was offered in a cup and decorated very nicely with the topping. I really like how they put toppings on the exterior and on the inside of the ice cream. After all, I am paying $1 extra and I didn't feel jipped. The ice cream was good, but a bit on the creamier side. I'd love to try the other items.

This is the second time that we have been to Jeni's. The ice cream here is pretty good but they make fresh waffle cones that you can smell down the street. My favorite is the brambleberry crisp but they have a number of good flavors. I haven't tried any others as I stick to my favorites. This is a cool little place that has a line almost any time of day. The lines go pretty quickly so don't be discouraged. I really enjoy Jeni's and stop here anytime that I am in the area.

$3.50 for a Stromboli? That's a deal for lunch. It feels kind of weird that they charge you for marinara sauce to dip it in, though, like charging ketchup for a hot dog. Their week day staff are more grouchy than their weekend staff, I think.
Best tiramisu ever eatï¼ My husband and I really love itï¼Only problem is hard to find a parking sot.
This place is located at the linQ...we were walking by and stopped here to check it out... And got to try one of the best cupcakes ever!!!! So moist and so perfect!!

I've been a fan of Carlo because of his show Cake Boss so I was pretty excited to try his pastries out in Vegas. It is located inside of the Venetian across from his restaurant. We got there on a Saturday night around 10 PM and there was only a small line so the wait was not too bad. I would recommend not coming during this time because they were out of the two things I really wanted to try and their most popular items (Lobster Tail and Canoli). The selection was pretty limited at this time so I only got a California Cheesecake. I order/paid and then had to wait about 10 minutes to pickup my order. I didn't really understand this because all they have to do is box the Cheesecake. The cheesecake itself was alight but definitely not worth the price ($11) at all. I might come back to try the lobster tail and canolis but was not wowed at all by my first experience here.
Wow. Small shop with small selection of ice cream BUT really good. Lady working there extremely helpful. Fair prices for quality hand made ice cream. Hard to find but again well worth it.

We came here for the first time and the service was amazing and the owner was so nice! He was super friendly, and super passionate about his shop. He also let us try everything we wanted and it was overall an exceptional experience! Also, got the donut stuffed with ice cream. BOMB!

For a girl who loves icecream. (I literally eat it every day) this was a dream come true. I felt like I was 8 years old again waving down the icecream truck just the icecream was 100 times better. I got the strawberry shortcake icecream macaron sandwich. first of all it was huge and secondly it is probably one of the most favourite things I have ever eaten. It's fun, it's good icecream and you want to go back to try all the things. 10/10 would go again.

Atmosphere: Milk is a small stand inside the Cosmopolitan. We thought the fluorescent sign in the front was cute and great for pictures! Service: The service was solid. The line to get the treat was a bit on the longer side, but other than that, we do not have much to complain about. Food: We ordered a cup of the cereal milk soft serve with toppings of sprinkles and cornflake crunch. This was such an aesthetic cup of soft serve that looked really tasty and refreshing; however, our mouths would say otherwise. The soft serve had very little taste and tasted vaguely of plain milk with a hint of old cheerios in it. It definitely wasn't the sweet or rich milky ice cream we were expecting. Additionally, the sprinkles and cornflake crunch were alright additions but did not elevate the dessert except for looks. Overall: While Milk had such a cute aesthetic both in terms of the stand and the dessert, our cereal milk soft serve was perhaps some of the most underwhelming soft serves we've ever had. The only reason we would come back is to try other desserts or to take a picture of the soft serve and then melt it to use as overpriced milk.

I could not have been more disappointed! My friend and I were SO excited to have the famous Sprinkles cupcakes while on vacation in Vegas this week. We each ordered 2 different flavors and couldn't wait to dig in...what a waste of money! All of them were the same quality...we both said they tasted like week-old, stale cupcakes that wouldn't have been good when they were fresh. The frosting was too rich (which for me to say is something) and the cake in each one was dense, crumbly, just unpleasant. We are both huge fans of desserts, especially cake and cupcakes and both of us ate only about a third of each of the cupcakes before we sadly threw them away. We both agreed that we have had MUCH better cupcakes from our local grocery store. They are obviously just riding on their fame and know they don't care if they have return customers. So disappointed.
We went to get ice cream and ice cream sandwiches it was so delicious but the wait was so long I love it but I don't think I like waiting that long in line for ice cream but it was so delicious.

Okay for the price but the spinach bread didn't taste fresh. The guy with the beard was a little rude, he laughed at the allergen question i had and without looking into it said that eggplant parm doesn't contain eggs. When I asked him to please double check, it ended up having eggs. The two stars are for the price and the location.
Great ice cream and fun little shops they have with old dime store type of toys. Very cool little shop. I recommend the strawberry & coffee ice creams.

This place has me coming back all the time. The soft serve is still delicious, and I feel like the toppings/varieties are just continuing to grow. Pick a flavor that you love and you can't go wrong. Expect long waits in the evenings. My only complaint every visit was that there was no outside menu to help me decide which delicious treat I was going to indulge in. (I would usually have to peak through the windows to make a last minute decision). But Lo behold.
Summer in a take out box. So yummy and insanely hip part of Phoenix's late infill project to reclaim the downtown.

How this is not a five star for everyone is a damn shame This is a world class bakery EVERYTHING is awesome This is a place you must visit if your in the Venetian or Palazzo Macaroons will change your life Bakery not to be confused with the restaurant Of the same name up stairs

Though their creamy rich ice cream is the main draw, they're more of an all-around dessert shop, offering yogurt, sorbet, shakes, cookies, and even cupcakes. The proprietor is from the Bay Area, but unlike the many yogurt and sweet shops that have sprouted up around here, there is little competition in the Valley in terms of creating unique flavors--like their Cheese Course Duo or Sumatra Espresso Shot--made from homemade, all natural ingredients. People have flocked to try something, not only new and different, but good. They've become a thriving business since opening last summer. Now, why couldn't I have thought of that?

I decided on a cookie cup because of the many reviews I read. It wasn't too hard to find especially because I came during the day. Yes, it was in a building right next to a painting supply shop (I think). My cookie cup consisted of coffee ice cream, Oreo topping, caramel macaron and a toasted marshmallow. It was delicious and loved every bite. I wish I didn't share with my hubby because to me, it's only enough for one, not to share. Next time, I'm not sharing. The prices are above average- with my additional topping, it came out to be close to $8 including tax.

Not often do I give 5 stars but this place is up there! They have a good selection of product and it's all delicious. My wife and I grabbed an ice cream filled donut. We had the animal cracker and Cookie Monster. The ice cream is really made with natural products. I did not end up with that bloated feeling or sweet after tastes that other ice creams leave behind. Besides the delicious treats, the owner was super nice and took his time explaining the menu. The women before us sampled all the flavors, which was the fing annoying but, he was courteous and very patient with them. Will definitely go back!

The ice cream is good, the dough is amazing, but Brandon is the BEST ice cream shop owner. My husband and I couldn't stop talking about what an awesome experience we got out of buying ice-cream. Brandon is so passionate and patient and made us feel very welcomed and definitely wanna come back! Don't miss this place as I'm sure you will feel the same way and come back and write a 5-star review :)

Disappointed. Just had a red velvet cupcake and I've actually had better from a grocery store bakery at home. The cake was very bland and the abundant icing was just saccharin sweet with no flavor aside from pure sugar. I grew up in NJ and hope that Carlos' more traditional confections are better, because this cupcake was an embarrassment to my heritage.

I mean, really, who doesn't love cupcakes? I'm guessing you probably do since you're reading this. If you don't like cupcakes you are crazy! Sprinkles doesn't disappoint when it comes to their cupcakes. They are tasty and feel like they are fresh out of the oven. They have different cupcakes that are featured every day and a few that are available every day. They also have season cupcakes. At this location they also sell ice cream (wahoo!). Their ice cream availability is similar to their cupcakes. I decided to get the sprinkles sundae. This is a cupcake with ice cream in the middle. I decided on vanilla bean ice cream and vanilla chocolate hazelnut cupcake. It really hit the spot and satisfied my craving. The downfall for a local is that it is on the strip, it would be awesome if they opened another location that isn't. But for now I'll take the trip to the linq for these awesome cupcakes.

Love the unique ice cream flavors, they offer flights so you can get a sampling of multiple flavors too. Check out the seasonal offerings as well. Staff is super friendly and knowledgeable of the flavors. They also offer pints (pricey though), water, and apparel items too.
Great ice cream spot, try the salted butter caramel sauce. Can't wait to go back again soon!

Very good ice cream but I was not blown away by it considering the line. The place is small and the line spills down the sidewalk. The smell is what really gets you as they make fresh waffle cones right as you walk in, yummy! I appreciated the creative choices but was not wowed by the flavors.

I have been to melt a few times. This last trip was terrible! From the minute I walked in, I felt pressure to make a choice immediately. The staff just stared at you. Less than helpful. I tried a few flavors and decided on the peanut butter. MEH!! Simply not that great. Also, stupid expensive. Not worth it. If you're in the downtown area I would recommend sweet republic, or churn. Much better service and ice cream is way better there. Not pleased and will not be coming back!

Amazing quality of ice cream. Rotating flavors can be a bit odd sometimes so be sure to sample anything like "sweet potato, bacon, ale, mustard, etc." type flavors because it will probably have the label's real ingredient within and taste like it. This place is amazing though! I like getting a triple play and enjoying every bite! Best ice cream shop that I have visited in AZ! A+++++

When I moved to Charlotte from Chicago. I never knew how much baked goods meant to me. Until I visited AmÃ©lie's... This is the third location I've visited. I love how the quality has remained the same through out. The first time I went was with a friend who raved about their macaroons. I had to try it. I'm now addicted. I try them everywhere I go. AmÃ©lie's has the best macaroons IMO. Their iced coffees are strong. On one of my visits with my friend we opted to have caramel added to our remade drinks because it was almost on the bitter side. Which leads me into customer service. They treat you real good here. They are willing to go through flavors and heating up your treats when needed. I see why people of all ages and walks of life can be found here. The relaxing atmosphere and the bomb treats. AmÃ©lie's is a must when you're in Charlotte. I haven't tried their other baked goods but my friends say their awesome. Please check them out if you're looking for a treat...or two!
This is one of the best places to get fun shakes and ice creams. It was my first time visiting and a wonderful experience. I would definitely go back

Fatty Daddy's thing is the cookie cup, and it's awesome. Ice cream with a macaroon and a toasted marshmallow. It's really good, but a little pricey at $7 IMHO. That said, steer clear of the bubble tea. My wife loves bubble tea, and she said it was the worst she has ever had. I tried it and I agree. Tasted like extremely watered down milk and ice. It was really bad. Good thing the ice cream is awesome.

Terrible customer service. Overpriced, mediocre food (at best). Stopped by about 10AM on a Sat for a snack and some juice. There were 6 people working behind the counter, and every one of them was dismissive and unfriendly. They all seemed as if they hated their jobs. I get that customer service can be a frustrating job, especially in food, but I expect to be treated with kindness and respect. After I ordered and paid, they forgot my food. I had to ask for it about 10 mins later. The bottled orange juice was good. The toasted turkey and cheese baguette was not. Dry. Cold. Tough. Disappointing. Maybe their coffee and espresso drinks are better, but I'll never stop by there again to find out.

I'm not sure how I had never heard of Handel's prior to last week, but alas someone told me about it and it didn't disappoint. I kidn of dig their set up. Just 4 walk up windows, no inside seating, but you can see where all the magic happens through the window. It is a little odd not looking at the ice cream as you make your selection. Given some of the odd names it can be tough to know what's what. However, I tried two samples and they were super cool providing them. Cost for a cone is less than Baskin Robbins and on Tuesday's I learned they have $2 scoops. If I lived closer I would definitely hit this place up on the regs. Being in the Henderson/Green Valley area that's just not possible. For my maiden voyage I went with an old classic, Cookies & Creme. Or I think it was Oreo chunk, something along those lines. The pics from fellow Yelpers is what put it over the top for me. They are not shy with the Oreos, which is how all versions of this ice cream should be. Super creamy, Oreos were soft. And while there were a lot, they were spread out, so you didn't just eat the Oreo then have none for the rest of the journey. It was perfect. My wife went with the Cookie Dough and it was disappointing. Not a ton of dough and a little blah-zay. She loved my selection though. Cool place, good ice cream. If in the area I'd definitely stop by!

I've been to Mitchell's and their other location for a very long time. This Mitchell's in particular is the best. They have all the workers behind the counter with all the packaging of the ice cream. The ice cream is one of Cleveland's classic. And lastly if I'm not mistaken, this is the only place that has the porter coffee ice cream. It's one of the Great Lakes brewery flavors I've only gotten here at this location. The only downside to this place is the parking situation. But other than that, I would recommend this place to anyone visiting Cleveland.
it was great, got a box of goodies and intended on taking them back home for my family to enjoy but for some reason they never made it, we ate them all!

HOLY MOLY the vegan creme brÃ»lÃ©e donut was the best thing sweet treat that's hit my mouth this week. I had their Hawaiian hazelnut drip coffee with almond milk and it was the best combo with my donut. This place sells out incredibly fast, so come first thing in the morning!!

I've never had donuts even close to as good as what you get at Jack Frost. The donuts themselves always have just the right texture, consistency, and flavor. Whether it's a glazed or a cake donut, you're getting something top-notch. And then there's the different toppings. I could talk about many of them, but do yourself a favor and get the donut with both the white and chocolate cream. I wish they sold that stuff in a tub so I could just go at it with a spoon. Plus, despite the fact they're often busy the service is always excellent. I go there every other week or so with my son before school, and they're always so nice to him (and half the time they give him his donut for free, which I never ask them to do). Just an excellent, old-school Cleveland institution.

We found Fatty Daddy because of their (smartly placed) ICE CREAM banner on the corner of Miller & Almeria. We checked the place out last night, which coincidently, was their grand opening. Even though they're not likely to have food trucks and a DJ on a regular night, we will be back. We had the Blueberry Muffin and Thin Mint ice cream flavors. Guess what they tasted like? Yup. Right on point, guys. Really, really luxe tasting ice cream base, too. Can't wait to come back!

This was such a cute little ice cream place, although it was a little difficult to get to because there was construction on the nearby roads! I really liked the swirly mural on the exterior of the building. The interior isn't anything special and very tiny -- no places to sit inside (there were several outdoor tables though), which was disappointing because it was 110 degrees outside. The ice cream: The flavor selection was definitely varied and featured several unusual flavors, like Thai tea, fruity pebbles, and ricanelas. I got the fruity pebbles and red, white, and blue sky (I visited on July 5th -- it consisted of strawberry, blueberry, and lemon) flavors and both were awesome! I especially liked the fruity pebbles one because it reminded me of eating cereal as a kid. The red, white, and blue sky was also delicious and not overpoweringly fruity, which was nice. My mom said that the ricanelas and Thai tea flavors were good, although she was miffed because the female cashier gave her less ice cream than the male one did! Side note: The male server was very friendly! My dad got the blueberry muffin flavor, which wasn't anything special, and my brother got the chocolate and cherry flavors, which he said were good! Presentation: I thought it was cute how they serve ice cream in Chinese takeout containers with fortune cookies instead of the standard cup! I found it kept the ice cream from spilling as much as it does when it's in a cup and the servers can be more generous in portion sizes. Price: For a double scoop, it was $5.25, which isn't too bad for such cool flavors! I would definitely come back!

This is a very popular place. There was a lot of foot traffic in and out which was a good sign. They have a simple menu and easy to understand. Ambiance inside was nice and bright. Seating is limited. There is outdoor seating but very limited as well. The staff were great. They explained the menu what the new flavors were and the wait time for everything. They provide samples if needed and the amount of ice cream is a good amount. The shakes are great even the coconut I was great in place of vanilla. It would be good to have more flavors available for the shakes. There are a good amount of choices for the boba. Looking forward to coming
Great coffee and pastries. The Cafe Creme is my favorite when I'm visiting from Seattle.
Worst cheesecake I've ever had in my life, texture was horrible and tasted even worse.
Really cute. I was a bit disappointed to find out that all the yummy baked goods they have on their website are rotated in and out, but really enjoyed what we did get!

This bakery deserves 5 stars! Everything is so reasonably priced and such high qualitty!!! Amazing customer service too. They have savory and sweet pastries. You grab your own set of tongs and help yourself. Make sure you check out the refrigerated case too - the caramel pudding and strawberry cake is so good!

DONUT have one!!! LOL. Amazing donuts. The crime brÃ»lÃ©e donuts are so fun to crack open, but my fave was the red velvet donut. Worth the Ã¼ber ride to get there. My 2 wishes... Closer to the strip and if they deliver to California.
Vietnamese coffee and vanilla are very good! And the ambiance is very cute.

Cozy, eclectic environment perfect to study in. My favorite pastries are the strawberry tarte, palmiers, and vanilla macarons. The pastries are expensive which is my only complaint but I keep going back so it must be worth it!
Looks great and smells awesome. everything was so quaint from the size of the pastries and chocolates to the french eclectic decor. I was very statisfied for breakfast and coffee

Went here with the girlies for the first time on sunday to help Sarah get over her cheating ex and it was such a good idea. Some of the best ice cream that we have ever had. Might need to make this a weekly thing and come every sunday! Only suggestion is that milkshakes should be an option.

I use to live down the street from Donut Bar. Their donuts were great! Full of flavor, fresh, fluffy, and versatile. As long as you like donuts, you can for sure find something here to your liking. Their iced chai was also very good. It was a little pricey for how watered down it seemed, but overall had a good sweet flavor.
This place is amazing!!! The workers are sweet and even put little toys on our kids ice cream! What a special place with great seating in the back.

I can't stop coming here, and it doesn't help that this location is minutes away from my home. I really love that the ice cream is made in-house, and the story that goes behind this business. I like to think that I'm somewhat of an ice cream connoisseur (I mean, what ice cream lover wouldn't think so of themselves), and the sweet treats here are TO DIE FOR. I can't tell you what my favorite is because they all are (except for maybe grape). When bananas foster is around, that would probably be my go-to, but graham central station is so unique and delicious that it would be my overall new favorite ice cream flavor. Definitely stop by, even if the line is long, because taking home a pint is worth it.
WOW! So good. Definitely the best ice cream in Vegas, may be the best ice cream anywhere. We travel all the way from Centennial Hills to go here.

This place is on the second floor of the Cosmopolitan Hotel and Casino in Vegas. Back in June when I came here I saw this place but didn't try it cause I wasn't in the mood. This trip in Nov. this time I decided to try something after seeing the pics and reviews and then asked the gal who was taking my order what was popular and/or what was her favorite she mentioned a few things from the menu but she mentioned the strawberry plus corn milk quakes which some people mentioned in reviews so she explained what it was and I decided to get it it was good. I originally was gonna get a shake but I wasn't feeling any of the flavors on the menu so the quake was the choice. It was pricey though but everything on the menu was pretty pricey but what the heck it's Vegas. I'll try a shake next time.

Wow! I was not expecting for an ice cream shop to blow me out of the water. I decided to give this place a try because I've heard so many good things about their different ice cream flavors made in house. First, the service was fantastic! They made sure the patio was spaced out so when people went up to get their order no one was stepping on each other's toes. The team Novel Ice Cream did a great job answering all my questions and allowed me to try a few flavors before I made my decision. They also shared with me what was gluten-free on the menu, including the little biscotti that comes with the smaller ice cream cups. I know when I go back to Phoenix, I am definitely coming here.

A friend and I came here right before closing on a Thursday night. This was my first time going but I've always been curious because every time I pass by there's always a huge line! We waited in line and I tried to read the extensive menu through the window behind the counter, which was pretty difficult. My friend informed that there menu is actually online so that might be an easier way to look at the huge menu. I decided on a vanilla cone dipped in cherry. I ordered a medium and was surprised how big it was! However, the cherry coating left a little bit of a waxy taste in my mouth. The vanilla ice cream was delicious though! While I don't think think I'd try their dip cones again I'd definitely give it another go with one of their many other products. Customer service was great! Even though it was close to closing and still had a line the woman who took our order was super friendly and helpful. Also this ice stand only takes cash but there is an atm on site.

Looking for a bite to eat in Ohio City? I highly recommend stopping by Crepes De Luxe located in the West Side Market. You definitely get what you pay for in terms of flavor and filling your stomach. Whether you have a sweet or savory tooth, they have a variety of ingredients to satisfy your craving. They have a menu to choose from but you're more than welcome to make your own. I usually go for the savory crepes. For an extra tip, if you ask for salt they use the pink Himalayan salt which makes a huge difference for flavor than just regular table salt.

The good: pastries are good. Fresh baked and well prepared. They have a lot of choices from savory to sweet and even the cake slices are well made. The mango creme brulee is a good choice; soft chiffon cake and slight notes of mango makes it a satisfying bite. Service is good, at least in how knowledgeable the staff is and how they expedite the baked goods to front of the house. I would go back for the pastry, which for a bakery is a good thing. The bad: i do want to point out that the bad is not really pointed to anybody in particular because as I understand it, the bakery is a popular chain in California (LA maybe) so I understand corporate marketing strategy and whatnot. But it honestly angers me that every pastry is individually wrapped in plastic. You enter the bakery, get a tray, stack on the pastries, and they will individually wrap the pastry on its own. Even if you ask for a bag or a box, the staff said that it is their policy to waste the plastic. This is done whether you dine in or not. The sustainability of it is ridiculous. Again, it's not the staff's fault but the establishment itself. This individually wrapping technique thing is wasteful and honestly contributes to the long lines that everyone is reviewing about. Taking the time to do that makes a two minute transaction into seven minutes. It just angers me that this idiotic policy is in place as if it keeps the integrity of the pastry by doing that. So yes, the hype is understandable and the pastry is worth a visit. But carbon footprint you leave and the waiting in line you have to suffer through are not.

The best ice cream donuts anywhere. They change ice creams pretty regularly but always keep a few standard flavors. You can get ice cream by the scoop but the donut or waffle ice cream is where it's at. We go whenever we are in the area and sometimes make the 30 min drive just for this place

Was excited to discover a Carlo's Bakery across from the hotel I was staying at. Planned to hit it first thing in the morning for my much needed coffee and a pastry or pastries...don't judge. Got there about an hour after they opened and was pleasantly surprised on my a few people ahead of me. The line took longer then I thought it should take govern the amount of people but I was ok with it as I was able to fully decide what i wanted when it was my turn. Everything looked absolutely delicious. After resisting to order one of everything I settled on mini connolis, cannoli tart, peanut butter chocolate cupcake, and a variety of cookies. EVERYTHING was delicious. Melted in your mouth good. Minus a star for not having coffee. Apparently the machine wasn't working? ? Bakery with no coffee is just a sin in my opinion. Next time I'm in this neck of the woods I'll be back to try a few more things. Hopefully they get the coffee thing worked out.

I've been to Sloan's a couple more times since I posted my original review, and those visits were pretty disappointing. First, there seem to be less employees working than there were the first time I cane here. Needless to say, there were no employees offering to give samples of their ice cream on those visits. Also, it's not very clear where to line up to order or ask for a sample, since the registers are in the middle of the counter, rather than at one end of the store or another. And they don't seem to have their Chewy Pralines fudge anymore :( I tried their Kitchen Sink brownie (chocolate, peanut butter chunks, butterscotch pieces, toffee, and caramel) which I found to be moist and tasty the first time I had it. However, it was hard and didn't have enough caramel or toffee pieces in it on my next visit. Please bring back the Chewy Pralines fudge and have clear designation of where the line begins!

I don't understand all the hype. Maybe I had high expectations, after all I am a regular at The Cupcakery! But anyway, I had the red velvet & the triple cinnamon cupcakes. The red velvet was not impressive to me, the frosting was too sweet and the cake itself just lacked flavor. I will say that the cinnamon one was pretty delicious though! Service was good but overall it's not a place I would seek out if I were in need of sugar. Maybe next time I'll try the ice cream, which I hear is a better option!

I finally had the chance to try Sprinkles cupcake and there was not a long line like the ones in California. Maybe it was my lucky day in Las Vegas. On one side, they sell cupcakes and the other side, they sell ice cream. I think it's not bad that it's $3.75 a single scoop, but I rather try the cupcake first. A cupcake cost $3.75 each, which is a good and decent price for a dessert. There are such a large variety of cupcakes, but I had the red velvet and the vanilla. Both were delicious. It's located across from Ghiradelli Chocolates and you can see the high roller ferris wheel while enjoying your cupcake.

I had heard about this place for awhile now but had yet to make it out there...and boy oh boy I am so glad that I did I will definitely be coming again soon. Butter butter everywhere. I would trade chocolate any day for a baguette and butter. At Amelie's you don't have to trade in your chocolate but please dig your teeth into one of their classic french sandwiches like the croque monsieur and don't leave until you have a pastry... how about a chocolate croissant to go. I really wanted a taste of everything, since they're open 24 hours I probably could have done it. They also have a great espresso bar, I was really thirsty so I went with a lavender lemonade that was delicious and refreshing. Relax by yourself, or bring your friends. A very unique place and well worth every penny. .

We rushed in 5 minutes before closing and had no clue what to order. It was my first time there, we're visiting from CA. The two young ladies were very friendly and helpful, they gave us some great suggestions. We got 2 German chocolates, a lemon cheesecake and a toasted coconut. The donuts were fabulous! We will definitely be back and we appreciate the great service.

Love this little spot, super cute, locally owned, it has little board games, the place is a hidden gem. The owner is the cutest thing! This hit the spot with my sweet tooth. We got 2 cookie cups (marshmallow, macaron and gelato) and omg we loooved every bite.

I was really looking forward to trying Milk Bar, but to my dismay, it really wasn't that great. It's not even that the prices are ridiculous or anything; the sweets just didn't live up to the hype. When we went around 1:30pm on a Sunday, there was about 10 people in line and it didn't take too long to get to the counter. They ran out of a few of the popular cookies so we ended up getting the crack pie, corn cookie, blueberry and cream cookie, birthday cake truffles, and the cereal milk soft serve with cornflake crunch. The crack pie was way too sweet for my taste, though I enjoyed the consistency of the pie.. The blueberry and cream cookie was kind of oily, and the corn cookie was pretty good. I only had a bite of the soft serve because I was so full, but my first impression was slight disappointment and that I've had better. So all in all, I may have expected too much, but it was simply a mediocre experience.

Almond Croissant - It was hard and over baked. I had huge hopes but was very disappointed. This must be fluke, must be a one time mistake because I saw a picture of one of their half eaten croissants with airy hole pockets. But if they had messed up a batch, why not just throw it out? Where is the quality control? It really is not worth tainting their brand name.

Babcock Dairy ice cream from The Daily Scoop savored on the terrace is THE definition of summer in Madison! I wholeheartedly give this a five star review on the product itself. The view of the terrace, that it's a locally made product (like not food miles but food feet practically), that the purchase supports the university, the reasonable prices ($3.20 for small), a variety of flavors not *too* crazy but not so "vanilla" as to be blasÃ©, they are all reasons that contribute to this five star rating. Look for it inside the union in the first floor, with a stand outside near the doors weather permitting. Allow 15 minutes to stand in line, minimum during peak times. It's worth it! Sit down on the lakeshore steps with your cool treat for a chill out that is uniquely Madison! And orange custard chocolate chip all day every day!! Call me when you're going!
Favorite ice cream in the Burgh! I would eat it year round if I could. Key Lime Pie! Can't wait till the warmer weather.

The donuts here are to die for!! Wow! I couldn't believe how amazing and different they were. And the coffee...wow!!! Do yourself a favor and head down here! You won't regret it. the staff is super sweet, helpful and full of spirit!!! Best donut shop in Vegas!

I love love love the ice cream here! I am in town visiting, found out the snap-o-lantern(pumpkin ice cream with ginger snaps) was available and made a trip over. I also love the green tea, blue moon, zoreo and horchata. It is always difficult to make a decision, but it always has to be in a waffle cone. My one complaint about the place is the service tends to range from aloof to snotty.
Oh my... the focaccia. The FOCACCIA! It's incredible. The rosemary, olive oil, and sea salt on the soft, crispy crusted bread, is divine. Definitely check out this bakery.
Also, should people be taking the bakery items, placing them on their tray, and then putting them back? And why does the cashier deal with money and handle cakes without a glove?

I love all of their delicious treats. The food is always yummy and fresh. The staff is polite and well informed about the products. Unfortunately there is normally very long waiting line, fortunately the staff is very fast so they got what i thought was going to be a 45 minute wait into a 15 minute wait.

First time here. Straight from the airport. Saw the hype on yelp and facebook. No line and plenty of parking. Close to downtown. Only got a few.. their creek brÃ»lÃ©e is a signature item. Hard crispy sugar shell. Yeast insides with custard filling. Looked good. I had the strawberry pop tart which has an actual pop tart inside. Yeast around the pop tart. It was good but not the best ever. A bit dry. This put them on the map. Had the Meyer lemon blueberry cake donut. Wow! It was delicious. I could've eaten maybe 5. Crispy edges. Moist and soft inside. Bursting with blueberry flavor in the cake. Nice lemon flavor icing. Not too sweet. I would get again if in walking distance. It's a bit overwhelming as there are so many options and I wanted to try so many. Also got a Hawaiian coffee and it was very tasty. Staff was very friendly. Recommend if you are a donut fan.

This was super cute spot!! It's in little tiny shed looking houses and it has other cute places. Anyways upon entering the little area where the venue is, there was a small farmers market and music. From there you walk in to this tiny place and they walk you through the flavors and what to do and samples. They have several flavors and toppings. Vegan choices too!! I had the raspberry sorbet and it was pretty incredible. Got my ice cream right away and on the Side of the little house there was a seating area. Overall it was cute, outside of the walls it's a little weird. I got a little weird ambience. However the place is super adorable and I do recommend it.

Had the sweetest experience at Novel! Was visiting Phoenix for the weekend and we had to try this place out. The ice cream flavors are unique and they allow you to sample once you get up to the window. I got a scoop of the carrot cake flavor in the donut, topped with Cinnamon Toast Crunch cereal. A little bit of a messy treat but the flavors were amazing. The best part was the service -- we had admittedly arrived quite late and close to their closing time, and the line continued long past our party as well. Even after closing, we were served with a smile and with the utmost patience. The girl at the window was so helpful and made conversation with each customer, and overall it was such a pleasant experience. If I'm ever back in Phoenix, I will be back!!

Butterwood did an amazing job with our wedding cake on October 28th! It looked and tasted fabulous! We opted for a pumpkin buttermilk cake with cream cheese frosting and it was delicious!! The fresh floral decorations were also beautifully done. We are saving a piece in the freezer for Christmas and we cannot wait to dig into it again! They were kind enough to deliver the cake to our venue on the day of the wedding, and their prices were very reasonable. I would highly recommend them to anyone who is looking for a beautiful and unique wedding cake - we received so many compliments on it!!

A good place to stop anytime for a cool, sweet treat on one of the warm or simmering days in Phoenix/Scottsdale. I ordered the Cookie Daddy - your choice of ice cream and macaroon topped w/ melted marshmellow. I chose salted caramel ice cream and vanilla macaroon. Nice touches of sweet, salty, warm and cool. So delicious! You can sample the various ice cream flavors before making a decision and the staff was very friendly and patient. Both bonuses. Stop by and give them a try.
Hands down the best ice cream/sorbet. It is also available at Whole Foods if you have time constraints.
Ice cream was old and with ice. Waffle cone wasnt tasty, I didn't finish and put it in trash bin. $10 ice cream for tourist, taste disgusting.

I was so excited to taste a piece of the rainbow cake with sprinkles topping, I paid $10 for a small slice. The cake looks beautiful unfortunately the taste of the cake was exceptional disappointing. Sad when a famous chef fails to deliver a quality product.

The good: service was great! The Filipino girl at the front line is a great ambassador. She's really warm and super helpful. And despite getting our drink order wrong, she was apologetic and quickly made good on their error. The not so good: the donuts are okay. Great novelty flavors, names and combos. But none it them really knocked our socks off. They make for great photos but none of them made me feel like I had to go out of my way to come back here. I didn't realize it's the same franchise as the Donut Bar in San Diego.

I came in the place assuming that I will come out feeling like crap because most dessert places serve humongous portion. Surprisingly this place didn't made me feel like that! For me the portion was right. Not only that, the ice cream is worth the calories because it's just that good! I had the Maltesers flavor ice cream with the red velvet macaroons and melted marshmallow on top. And it literally tasted like melted cold maltesers! Me and my coworkers found our favorite dessert spot in Scottsdale!

Take a chance and try the blue cheese with honey! As spoiled as I am with the amazing San Francisco Ice Cream Scene (Bi-Rite and Humphrey Slocombe) this is one of the best scoops I have ever had. Such inventive flavors, well exectued classics and an incredibly nice staff. It's a new must-do every time I'm in Phoenix visiting the family.

This is kind of teetering on a two star rating but the ice cream was pretty good and the service was fantastic so I swung it up to three. At this price, up-charging for waffle bowls that are so stale that they are kind of gross is not cool. I pay about the same per treat at Ghirardelli and they have never given me anything that was even slightly less than perfect. This was my first and possibly last visit to this place and the waffle bowls were so disgusting that we ended up throwing them away after paying extra for them. It's too bad because they're in a great location and we really did like the ice cream. I'm a dark chocolate fanatic and they had multiple options for me. I guess you can get it in a non-edible bowl but that kind of sucks the joy out of it.

I came here every week overall I enjoyed the environment and service every time but I had a bad experience today. I feel like I'm not respected by the barista/cashier - Jesse (that's what she told me not sure if she made it up). I'd like some milk for my dripped coffee but she did not seem like to help me with it. She was reluctant to get me the milk but instead trying to have me used the cream on the table. That is not something I need and when I insist on getting the milk, she took the milk out from the fridge with a lot of aversion on her face. I feel like being discriminated as she treats other caucasian customers with a MUCH MORE friendly manner. I feel like your store needs an ethical training especially for Jesse.

Save your money! I have heard rave reviews of Sprinkles cupcakes. I also watched every episode of Cupcakes Wars which the creator of Sprinkles cupcakes judges (now I think I will rethink her opinions). I got the cinnamon "cupcake"...I use the term cupcake loosely since mine tasted more like a dense muffin. Even if I poured milk directly on it I doubt it would be moist. My friend ordered the vanilla cupcake with milk chocolate frosting, also nothing to write home about. Overall I was thoroughly disappointed since I LOVE sweets. There are plenty of other local cupcake shops that I would choose over this chain. If someone bought me another cupcake from Sprinkles I would try it in hopes of redeeming the name but I will not be spending my money on these MUFFINS again. Sorry Sprinkles....step it up!

Presti's is one of the most popular bakeries in Little Italy area and in Cleveland. In the morning, they have a great selection of breakfast pastries like danishes, cinnamon rolls, pecan rolls, breakfast sandwiches and more. In the display case, they have various sweets such as cookies, brownies, cakes, cannolis, and more. They are most known for their Cassata cake (my mother's favorite cake of all time) which is filled with slices of strawberry and custard. My favorite items to get are the tiramisu and the traditional cannoli. Based on trying a variety of menu items, I feel like most of them tasted great, while some of them were a bit of a miss (I felt that the breakfast sandwiches taste a bit too dry for me, and some of the smaller cookies are mellow in flavor). Overall though, I love stopping by here for a quick sweet fix with friends and family!

First time visiting on a recommendation from a local. The store is beautiful, clean, and large. However the layout could be more efficient. There was not a large crowd so it was easy to move around but it would have been a crazy mess if there were even 10 more people. The concept is fun; grab a tray and tongs and take your pick of dozens of choices of sweet and savory pastries. My group had a great time loading our trays. I appreciated the labels with allergy ingredients but would love to see more of a description of the item also. And several bins were missing labels completely. Overall taste was good but not great. The chocolate cake was dry and needed more cream. The croissant is flaky and chewy with just enough chocolate. The potato and cheese pastry was not very flavorful and the filling was mushy. I really liked the chocolate hazelnut muffin but it had only one small dollop of hazelnut cream. Nothing is overly sweet including the chocolate cream cheese bun. The packaging is excessive and wasteful. Either change to recyclable paper bags or just use boxes. It was a fun experience but I don't know if I'll be back the next time I'm in town.

Love Amelies!! The Noda location is great!! The other locations are not a good. I always get the salted caramel brownie and lavender lemonade. Whenever I have guests in town, I always take them there. It is a perfect place to sip coffee and spend time with friends. Seriously love Amelies

It was my first time to Melt. And wow! Most delicious, amazing ice cream...the Cookie Monster, oh my! However, I have to say the customer service killed my overall experience. Music was jamming with a line out the door, but once you got inside you weren't made to feel welcomed. The two working didn't appear to have any excitement serving the anxious and excited customers making the nights most important decision; what flavor?! They had their heads down on their phones for the majority of check out. There was no "hey guys welcome to melt! Let us know if you have any questions." Or "thanks so much for coming in tonight...we'll see you back soon!" "Is this your first time in? If so we have punch cards." The only conversation I had with the girl was "make sure you bring your flight tray back inside." My dad has owned a restaurant for 30 years and from my experience I have learned that customer service can make or break your overall experience. Those working for you are supposed to represent your business in the most positive way possible; you kind of expect that being in the downtown Roosevelt district where people represent a chill, cool, and friendly vibe. With all of that said, keep making delicious ice cream at Melt! The flavors are inventive and who doesn't love the take-out containers with the crunch of the fortune cookie to end the party in your mouth. Here's to more happy scooping employees! #lovetheicecreambutnotheonesservingit

I can't even begin to recount the endless amounts of times that I've been to the Chocolate Shoppe. When I moved to Madison I was certain that when the winter hit I wouldn't want ice cream anymore, I mean when it's below freezing outside, why would you want something equally as cold? Well that was just wrong. Completely wrong. When a sweet tooth hit during the summer I went for the Chocolate Shoppe. And during the winter: still chose the Chocolate Shoppe. They have endless amounts of choices that use every sweet thing you could think of and sometimes it's even all combined into one flavor. Taste a few when you get there to make sure you get a good grasp of how awesome this place is. They have a hilarious motto when you walk in the stores that says something to the effect of: don't even ask about the nutrition because they aren't making these flavors to be healthy, they're making them to be delicious. And they've done that perfectly. Since Wisconsin has more than enough dairy to go around, the Chocolate Shoppe creates the creamiest ice cream I have ever had. You really shouldn't go anywhere else for ice cream if you come to Madison. Everywhere else is some sort of chain or self serve froyo place that just can't compete with the Chocolate Shoppe. Well done Chocolate Shoppe, well done.

I don't know much about the show but I'm sure the guy is great and successful. My Aunt has been to the original bakery since the 90s and has said good things. This place needs to get their act together. There is one line to wait on to pick out what you would like. When you get up to the counter and someone helps you there are no descriptions, no prices for 90% of the items. I know this is going to be expensive because it's on the strip so I go ahead and get some things - black and white cookies, canoli, cream puffs, chocolate chip cookies and a 3-layer cookie - which is my favorite and a holiday tradition for me. I was very excited for this 3 layer cookie to compare it to my own. The employee puts everything on a tray, asks my name and tells me to wait in another line to pay and get my baked goods. ANOTHER LINE. I see them place things in boxes and wrap them up the NY way in red and white string which is great but I'm not getting 2 dozen italian cookies and I could for-go the boxing because I was handing out the treats to my family in 10 minutes. So we wait and wait and wait some more, around 20 minutes to pay. The poor cashier needs to go to a line of waiting orders, type in everything the person ordered and ring them up, then call the person's name - which is horrible inefficient. Overall, it took probably 40minutes from standing in line to finish and they forgot my 3 layer cookie which cost a fortune. Never, ever go there. Save yourself for a true italian bakery in NY. This place is waste of time and money.

It's always ice cream o clock for me! And Page is the reason why! A family owned and operated treasure, Page is one of the oldest soft serve places in Pittsburgh. I took my parents here and they loved it! We ordered a vanilla chocolate twist topped with nuts, coffee soft serve and blueberry soft serve. They were all delicious! The best part is that it's not very sweet and heavy. The portion size is good and not overwhelming. We ordered small size with waffle cone. There has always been a queue but it's a fast moving one. Great staff as well. There is enough place in front of the store to park and chill. HIGHLY RECOMMEND!

PROS: -convenient downtown location -fast friendly service -strawberry split donut is amazing CONS: -vegan donuts are more expensive than the non-vegan ones -loud music (like nightclub loud) which makes it hard to communicate orders or chat with friends -creme brulee donut is blah, & the strawberry split donut is pretty heavy which leaves one other vegan option. I wish they had better vegan selection (ie: simple options like glazed, eclair, sprinkle, etc) If they had better vegan options I would frequent this location weekly, for sure. I've only seen the vegan sprinkled donut on 2 occasions. So far the strawberry split & sprinkled is my fav

Hot and gross feeling in their building as the sun set. It was cooler outside. They really should run the AC more. The line was long, unorganized, and not moving so we went to Ghirardelli instead for ice cream.
First time here tonight and the ice cream was crazy good. You're frozen treat comes in a Chinese takeout box which is cool. You also get a little fortune cookie on top and that's always a nice touch

This is a go to spot for me when we come visit Charlotte. Open 24 hours, the food is always consistently decent (sometimes awesome), the vibe is cool and welcoming. Depending on the time of the day I feel the pastries are better. We came late at night and the display was somewhat empty but hey, beggars can't be choosers. Still a wonderful time!

I'm an ice cream nut, raised in a family of ice cream nuts, and I'm telling you right now this is the best ice cream in Phoenix. Both people working in the shop were friendly and incredibly knowledgeable about ice cream. They were enthusiastic about giving us samples and made great suggestions. As for the ice cream...if you're an ice cream nerd, obsessed with milk fat and mouth feel, this is the place for you. If you just like amazing ice cream, this is ALSO the place for you. I had a Dough Melt - that's a warm donut filled with ice cream (butter pecan) and a topping (cinnamon toast crunch). Changed my life a little.

We stopped by here to try them out after going to try Sheng Ramen in Ballantyne. I dig the decor and it's where the old TCBY used to be at the Arboretum. I decided to get the soft serve which is a bit pricey at $5.50 but it was still good. I got the mix of Matcha and Vanilla and realized I'm still not the biggest matcha person but it was very good ice cream nonetheless. Totally instagrammable too as there were a group of girls taking selfie pics galore with their treats. I'm not sure if it's worth driving 30 mins for me but I'll def be back to try one of their drinks when I'm in the area to eat because New Zealand Cafe is my jam.

9/21/21 - CLARIFIED DIRECTIONS TO GRAB AND GO BAKERY: 1. Park in the Venetian Self Park Garage 2. Take Elevators to Food court /shops level 3. Take the escalators down and make a sharp right or left to join line for the shop counter (line window started right across JUICE FARM) Idk if they recently changed the location, but previous reviews mentioned near the palazzo theater, guest elevators etc, but If you don't know where those places are then you might get frustrated like i did. I done lost my mind looking for this counter right behind the escalators that take you up to coffee bean(food court) /parking garage. Pastries were just as good as I remember from the restaurant and the boxes flew home well on my flight home. Standouts- kouighamann : the closest texture I've tried in the states to the ones in France. Dense and buttery- should not be flaky or falling apart into crumbs. Almond croissant: so toasty (even when cold) with good buttery almond filling. Bouchon: a hybrid between a brownie bite and toasted cannele lol. Got a few chocolate chips on the bottom too.

Novel ice cream is hands down one of the best spots in Phoenix! The owner Brandon is awesome and super friendly. The staff are always very friendly and welcoming too. I love that they use a lot of local ingredients in their ice cream and are very knowledgable about their product. My favorite is the honey lavender - so tasty! Can't wait to go back and try more flavors.

Located in the middle of a quiet neighborhood. Not a lot of parking (3-4 spots?) but there is street parking available. I was surprised by the wide range of baked goods they offered. They also had cakes along the wall that were beautifully decorated. We ordered an iced coffee and the bread pudding. The bread pudding was very moist and coffee was good. Overall, very nice decor and atmosphere.

Mmm...! This place is my favorite for ice cream. I've been to both the Chocolate Shoppe and Paciguio and they don't compare. I got the pumpkin pie and Union topia. Both had rich flavors and textures. The prices are right too. $3.25 for two BIG scoops. Yum.

If you want fresh delicious coffee and possibly a tasty treat to accompany said drink, you've found the spot. I love stopping by Amelies in general but stop by often in the morning before work. I can always rely on the "two Emilys" for a good conversation and a delicious coffee beverage. Emily E and F thanks for all you do every morning! If it were up to me you'd get a raise!

I have watched Cake Boss for years since I am a cake decorator and baker as well. Buddy has always awed me with his creativity which made me want to try his products. I had the chance when we were at LV last week, there was no line and they had tons of pastries to choose from. But there are no sign or price as to what the items were. So it's hard to choose more so. We opted to just try the cookies, m&m and chocolate chips and a Christmas sugar cookies. Got 3 of each and 1 cheese danish. I was so surprised though that all of it rang to more than $40. The kids liked the cookies but they were like any other cookies that you can buy from bakeries that costs less. I didn't like the cheese danish because it was too sweet and the pastry itself was a bit hard, I'd prefer the ones from Panera bread. Maybe next time when I get the chance I'll try the cakes. We didn't get any at the time since we had some cakes already from another bakeshop.

The line was long, but it moved quickly, and the pastries were lovely. My friend got a chocolate cheesecake that was destroyed by our walk back to the hotel, but was delicious nonetheless, and I finally got one of those lobster claws that I'd seen on TV a billion times. It was yummy. The cream inside had a distinctly alcohol taste about it (the almond perhaps), and the croissant was very flaky and hard, but yet somehow it still worked all together. Like everything in Vegas, it was very expensive, but it was worth it to no longer crave those tempting claws.

This place is AMAZING! Go early for largest selection. Extremely huge AND delicious donuts. Carmelized bacon cinnamon roll & the creme brÃ»lÃ©e donut were the two I tried. So good! The staff was very happy, welcoming and efficient. Also met owner Joe. Very kind to visit with us a few minutes. We are going back tomorrow to try more.

Most delicious ice cream I have ever had and I have had a lot of ice cream! I had the hangover helper; sweet cream ice cream, brandy, and frosted flakes. AMAZING! The double chocolate and strawberry were great as well.

Best ice cream place in the 412 without a doubt!! Being from Homestead I grew up on Nancy B chocolate chip cookies. And Paige's Nancy B cookie sundae is like heaven on earth It's worth the long line wait they usually have every night

I FINALLY GOT TO TRY THEIR CEREAL MILK ICE CREAM. Sorry for the caps, I was just that excited. I tried some of their goodies (corn cookie, cake truffles) before, but since I haven't made it out to NY since before Y2K, I had not been able to try the ice cream. Until now. I love milk flavored ice cream, which my fiance finds ironic because I don't like to drink milk. Furthermore, I love the milk leftover from my cereal bowl. The fact that they put it in an ice cream makes me so happy. I got the classic cereal milk ice cream with corn flake topping. NOTE: THIS IS NOT A SUPER SWEET ICE CREAM. If you're expecting a lot of sweetness, this is not for you. I really enjoyed it. If you're into the sweeter things, they have a fruity cereal milk ice cream which tastes like the milk leftover after Froot Loops or Fruity Pebbles. I tried the sample of this one, and it was good, but I preferred the original cereal milk. I also bought a dozen cookies to take home with me. There were literally 10 corn cookies and 2 compost cookies. Those corn cookies are my favorite cookies of all time. Period. They almost taste like a sweet cornbread. I LOVE them. I do want to come back and trie the crack pie next time I come back. It's on my list!

We came here because we loved the original spot in downtown San Diego and enjoy a good donut. My wife enjoyed some donuts and I sadly didn't have any because they do not offer a gluten free donut. I think their concept of making donuts and trying to sell out everyday is silly. They could be busy all day with fresh donuts if they had different model where they baked them fresh. But the wife was happy regardless so we will be back.
First timer!!! It was ok! I think the cake would have tasted better if it wasn't refrigerated. I like room temperature cake.

I cannot rave about this place enough. We went on a Sunday morning to pick up some breakfast pastries. It was a quaint and small shop but the selection of baked goods was out of this world. We were so tempted to get everything from the pastries to the cakes to the macaroons but forced ourselves to just stick with the chocolate croissant and the very berry muffin. These did not disappoint. The chocolate croissant was massive (yet I still could have eaten more because it was so good) It was so flaky and had the perfect amount of chocolate it in. The muffin too was the perfect texture and bursting with a rich berry flavor. The only bad thing about this place is there isn't anywhere to sit inside so you can continue to enjoy the delicious smells of the bakery.
The most delicious ice creams ever!!! They are made right on the back of the store, and the flavors are based on local products availability. Cute place. Super friendly staff!!

I really love the food here. The flavors are amazing. There is a large variety of choices deli style from entrees to deserts. The staff makes me laugh. They are not necessarily the friendliest but they remind me of Seinfeld with the various characters and so I actually enjoy the personalities. Just don't take their rush personally. There is always a line to the door and it's not easy trying to take care of the customers as fast as they come for the food here. Normally I would give this a 5 stars. The only reason I can't is that for some reason, my gut is sensitive to something that is being used in the food. If it was a one time thing thenbi wouldn't say anything but it happened nthreeb try imes over a month and I cannot go back no matter how much I love the food. Sometimes the best tasting food does this to my gut. There are a few other restaurants that I have had the same reaction to multiple times and it's disappointing not to be able to go back without personal repercussions.

We were here 5/19/2017 for a small event with "Minions" from "Despicable Me movies". While were we waiting I could see into the kitchen where Employees who were making the ice cream wore, hairnets, beard-nets and gloves. This made us very happy to see and also that use local dairy products from Ohio Farmers. We were disappointed about the Employees at the Ice cream Counter were not wearing hairnet or gloves. Mitchell's Ice Cream is the Best ice cream I have had. I ordered Bing Cherry and chocolate chunks. The cherries tasted fresh and the chocolate chunks were large.

All of their pastries are certainly tasty, but they are priced way above what you would be able to get anywhere else in Charlotte - I understand that they are homemade and high quality products come with a higher cost to make, but the few times I've been here I've been pretty shocked at my receipts when I'm just buying one or two things

Weight watchers beware, this place is simply full of sweets and fattening ice cream sandwiches! You have to admit though that it's a good idea to Treat Yo'self every once in awhile. For those occasions where only delicious desserts will satisfy, I couldn't recommend Ninety's enough! If you're familiar with the boardwalk in the university area then you probably already know where it's at. Park on the side closest to the Light Rail and look for the Chinese restaurant, it's across the walk from there. While there's not much to talk of concerning the decor, it is very clean and there's a bit of seating for people looking to beat the heat. It seems that they're banking on most enjoying their treats on the boardwalk, the rest of the building quickly fills up with excited families and individuals alike wanting a delicious snack. So as far as the treats, what tickles your fancy? They have macaron ice cream sandwiches, ice cream filled doughnuts, ice cream cake pops, all kinds of desserts. You can also get your ice cream in a cup or cone, but why would you? The couple of macaron ice cream sandwiches I've gotten here were outstanding and well thought out. They offer different flavors of macarons paired with specific ice creams and toppings. The team even seems to have a thing for cereals, and incorporates them into a lot of their products! I have to admit, I didn't expect the macarons themselves to be such a high quality product since they're being used in ice cream sandwiches, but I'd say these were excellent macarons. They could even stand on their own with a traditional filling if they chose to do so. Maybe something to consider! I'd love give 'em the perfect score, but last time they were out of a LOT of the specials. They were very accommodating with substitutes, for example they were out of the samoa macaron and just did a regular vanilla on my sandwich. My girlfriend also wanted the coffee ice cream sandwich and they were out of its 'biscotti' macaron so she had to settle for a substitute as well. All in all it they were out of 3 macarons, and 2 ice creams. So I hope that next time I'm able to try some of their named creations as they intended. Still, a very good package either way. Not a gimmick, and still going strong. Way to rock, Ninety's!

Such a cute place! I love the ambiance and decor. Unfortunately I already bought a sweet drink in the area so I didn't get any ice cream here however the strawberry sandwich I ordered was yummy. I took a star off because it was super pricey and the bread was a little hard like it was left out too long. $4 for half a crust less sandwich with 1.5 strawberries and cream - it should've been made fresh.

I literally have been wanting to try this place for years. I made their recipe for birthday cake in 2014 and have dreamt of trying their stuff since. The staff is friendly, this review is solely on the food. I got a slice of crack pie and 2 cookies. Everything was meh, not mind blowing. Nothing's baked on site so it's not as good of quality as other bakeries. I microwaved my cookies so they were less hard and stale. The crack pie was decent. Honestly, I'd prefer safeway or sprouts lol. Because while the goods are average tasting, they're quite pricy. If you're able to microwave your cookies, they'll be a lot better, if not the cookies aren't very good as is lol. I also went the following evening and tried their birthday soft serve with cornflakes. It was really sweet and fun. I really want to try their birthday cake, I wish they sold it by the slice.

Omg the banana ice cream and the coffee chip are amazing! I went here today for my first time and tried the 4 scoop sampler. I also had the strawberry cheesecake and caramel truffle and they were great as well! Delicious!

Not only does this location have all the delicious cupcakes you've come to love from Sprinkles, they also have Sprinkles cookies and ice cream! We don't have these delicious additions at the location in Chicago so I was super excited. I had a Red Velvet waffle cone with Red Velvet ice cream and my husband had an Ice Cream Cookie Sandwich with one chocolate chip and one Snickerdoodle cookie with vanilla ice cream in between. Seriously, heaven.
Cool hipster-ish place, a great place for you to take a date to. Across the street is a even more hipster bookstore we took a walk in after our delicious ice cream.

Last time I visited this place was in 2015, and I had a cannoli and hot chocolate after the Rock n Roll Las Vegas half marathon! This time, I got a slice of the strawberry frosting rainbow cake, and UGH. This is what an excellent frosting-to-cake ratio looks like! It's a large slice, so it makes an extra special treat when you want something sweet. I gave four stars because I think the packaging could be nicer (shift from plastic to paper/cardboard).

One of the best places in Charlotte!! I just love everything abt Amelie's! Everytime I have a friend in town visiting we come here. The decor is eclectic & semi-Paris themed. Really fun. Open 24/7 & alway busy. I recommend not going weekend evenings because you will wait in line. The asparagus soup is great but I mostly just get dessert when I go. Coconut macaroons are great. Salted caramel brownies are heavenly. There are tiny chocolate cups w/ different fillings. The passion fruit is out of this world. Not to be confused with the chocolate passion fruit tho.

Ugh finally one opened close to me! Normally I always go for their taiyaki and never really cared about their soft serves -- I changed that today. Got their upside down ah-boong with Oreo and another one with their Milk Tea/Thai tea combo and it was so good! One can definitely not go wrong with their taiyaki here, as well -- Nutella, custard, and taro is my fave! They had an Oreo flavor in this location which is v new to me. I dig it. Please note that this location just opened so the line is friggin' long! Despite that, customer service is great!

Discovering Jeni's has completely changed the ice cream game for me. Every time I've gone to a store or bought a pint from Publix it's as if I've found a new favorite flavor - seasonal and staple flavors, I've never been disappointed. My go to has been to do both Gooey Butter Cake and Brambleberry Crisp together, it never disappoints. I love this location, and while there is always a line down the street, it's always quick and perfect for a sweet treat after visiting one of the restaurants nearby for dinner.

They had a red velvet cupcake that was Gluten Free so I had to try. It was good, there was a letter G on top, which I guess was to signify gluten-free. Customer service was great so I'm hoping the next time they'll have more options for me.

Tonight was my first time coming here and it was great! The people working the counter were very friendly and very willing to help you figure out what you might like. Also the ice cream flavors are crazy. I've never seen them before but they were awesome. They atmosphere was very cool as well with all the bright colors and the paintings on the wall. I would definitely recommend this place and will be going back for sure.

This is a solid donut shop. Lots of unique flavors are available, and it is obvious they put a little extra quality into the ingredients. I sampled two raised donuts, plain glazed and pumpkin spice, and one cake, strawberry with cream cheese frosting. They were good. Not the best donuts in the world per their claim, but good. I thought the raised donuts were a little too dense and chewy, but I enjoyed them nonetheless.

I have to admit I was a little hesitant going to the donut bar. After all, imho it had a large shoes to fill because of the prior tenant O Face Donuts. Sure, O face is now gone and one should not compare a defunct business to the next. But I cannot help it and I feel if you take the time to read what I mean, you may just end up agreeing. There is only one single difference between the two shops yet the difference is such a critical element. Creative originality. The Donut bar attempts to dazzle you with concoctions that are really just kids play. The creme brulee is creative in thought yet executed with no afterthought. Yea, the sugar is cool however only after one bite. Attempting to get through the constant crunch soon became annoying. I found myself trying to taste the center cream however once the sugar coating finally dissolved and my taste buds came to I found it to be just commercial custard filling. Very disappointing. The maple... O face had was one step ahead of everyone on this. When you go to a donut shop,no matter what part of the U.S. you go, you know what a maple bar taste like. It doesn't change and when you have one, you have had them all. O face however took the time to develop their own maple glaze which complemented the bacon. Donut bar..the same usual maple. The oreo cookie Jeez...how much sugar can you give? People, this is a regular glazed stuffed with an oreo cookie. mmmm Lard O face made donuts that were not only creative but containing other unique flavors. Mexican chocolate with horchata creme...yea, their own homemade horchata,not a tub filled with flavored gelatin goo Apple fritter. Dark. Happen to notice this to as the tray containing the fritters all looked the same,over done. To their credit, the inside was not overdone and actually the best of the group of donuts I had. In a nutshell, this is no O Face and probably never will be. I suppose this means I need to find something more on the level of my expectations. Which leads me to a final thought. Donuts that cost 2-4$ per should be on the level of artisanal. Otherwise, what am I paying for that I couldn't already get at Von's?

Love this place! As a family, we probably visit once a week. The team is fantastic and welcoming, the location is clean and adorable, the baked goods are delicious and the ice cream is great. No reason to go anywhere else!!

This is my go-to ice cream shop! Everything I've had is delicious. My favorites are Cap'n Crunch Berries, Cake Batter, Superfarm (Nutella), AZ Sunset (orange-cream), and Chocolate Brownie Fudge. The customer service is often lacking, and the ice cream is definitely pricey. But I just LOVE their ice cream, the location, their late night hours, and the fact that it's a local, independent business! I will be back again and again (and again).

Delicious. The pastries are unreal and truly remind me of being in France. The food is also very good but I truly recommend Amelie's for the pastries and sweets. Wonderful place with great treats and a fun atmosphere. Will definitely be back, whether that be at the Uptown location or their original location near NODA.
Unique and absolutely delicious! The cornflake ice cream has a perfect balance of sweet with a hint of saltiness that I LOVED!

A cute little donut shop! The creme brÃ»lÃ©e was delightful with its hard sugar shell top and yummy custard filling. The lemon pistachio donut was also surprisingly light with a great lemon flavor. They close when they run out, so get there early!

Really cool ice cream shop. I love the Chinese takeout boxes the ice cream is put in and the fortune cookie provided with it. Try to sample as many flavors as you can before making a decision. Great place to bring a friend or two. Can't hurt to visit.

We went here yesterday for honey lavender ice cream and watermelon sorbet. It was wonderful to get out for a little break and see everyone practicing social distancing and happy to have customers. It was a real treat. The sorbet was light and refreshing and the ice cream was rich and delicious. They are no longer giving samples so make sure you know what you want when you get there. They have lots of creative choices.

Dear sweet baby Jesus, this place is good! We started out with a scoop of half "salty caramel" and half "peanut butter and jelly" as our gateway drug at this deviously unassuming den of sugary sex appeal. I ordered the ice a fore mentioned ice cream and my girlfriend got a peanut butter, choco chip, pretzel cookie. The cookie was really good. The ice cream was spectacular! So we made our way to the end of this little strip off of the strip and I realized that I had lost all my ice cream.
